title: Pozole Rojo Red Hominy Soup

seo.title: How to Make Pozole Rojo Red Hominy Soup — Easy Mexican Recipe
seo.metaDescription: Learn how to make authentic Pozole Rojo Red Hominy Soup. This easy Mexican recipe includes step-by-step instructions, tips, and serving ideas.

easy Pozole Rojo Red Hominy Soup recipe
authentic Mexican Pozole Rojo Red Hominy Soup

seo.imageAlt: Authentic Mexican Pozole Rojo Red Hominy Soup recipe
seo.imageCaption: Delicious homemade Pozole Rojo Red Hominy Soup — a beloved Mexican classic

description: Pozole Rojo is a hearty and flavorful Mexican soup, traditionally made with hominy, pork, and a rich red chile broth. This comforting dish is a staple for celebrations and family gatherings, known for its vibrant color and complex, savory taste. Enjoy a taste of Mexico with this authentic and satisfying recipe.

ingredients.notes: roughly chopped, plus more for garnish

instructions.title: Cook the Pork
instructions.text: In a large pot or Dutch oven, cover the pork shoulder cubes with water and add half of the roughly chopped onion, 2 garlic cloves, and the bay leaf. Bring to a boil, then reduce heat, cover, and simmer for 45-60 minutes, or until the pork is tender. Skim any foam that rises. Once cooked, remove the pork and shred it using two forks. Reserve 2 cups of the cooking liquid.

instructions.title: Rehydrate the Chiles
instructions.text: While the pork cooks, place the dried guajillo and ancho chiles in a bowl. Cover them with hot water and let them rehydrate for 20-30 minutes, until softened. Drain the chiles, discarding the water.

instructions.title: Prepare the Chile Sauce
instructions.text: In a blender, combine the rehydrated chiles, the remaining half of the roughly chopped onion, the remaining 2 garlic cloves, Mexican oregano, and ground cumin. Add 1 cup of the reserved pork cooking liquid or chicken broth. Blend until very smooth, adding more liquid if needed to achieve a pourable sauce consistency. Strain the sauce through a fine-mesh sieve into a bowl, pressing on the solids to extract all liquid, then discard the solids.

instructions.title: Combine Ingredients
instructions.text: In the same pot used for the pork, add the shredded pork, rinsed hominy, and the strained red chile sauce. Pour in the remaining 5 cups of chicken or pork broth (or more reserved pork cooking liquid). Season generously with salt to taste.

instructions.title: Simmer the Pozole
instructions.text: Bring the mixture to a gentle boil, then reduce the heat to low, cover, and let it simmer for at least 30 minutes, allowing the flavors to meld beautifully. Stir occasionally to prevent sticking. The longer it simmers, the richer the flavor will become.

instructions.title: Serve and Garnish
instructions.text: Ladle the hot pozole into individual bowls. Serve with a variety of garnishes on the side, such as thinly sliced radishes, shredded green cabbage, finely diced white onion, fresh lime wedges, and diced avocado. Offer tostadas for scooping or crumbling into the soup. Enjoy this authentic Mexican delight!

tips: For a deeper, smoky flavor, lightly toast the dried chiles in a dry pan for a minute or two before rehydrating them. Be careful not to burn them.
Using a high-quality Mexican oregano is key to the authentic flavor profile of pozole. It has a distinct earthy, citrusy taste compared to Mediterranean oregano.
The garnishes are essential! Don't skip the fresh elements like radishes, cabbage, and lime, as they provide a crucial textural contrast and brightness to the rich soup.
article: Pozole Rojo Red Hominy Soup is a beloved Mexican Soups and Stews dish enjoyed across Mexican. This authentic recipe brings traditional flavors to your kitchen.
